headline: How The Amazon.com (AMZN) Narrative Is Shifting With Cloud Advertising And AI Investment Debates
summary: Amazon.com's updated fair value estimate of US$281.27, compared with the prior US$280.47, sits against a backdrop of active debate around where price targets should land. Some firms are edging targets higher on confidence in AWS, advertising and AI related investments, while others are trimming their numbers as they weigh higher capital needs and execution risks. As you read on, you will see how these shifting views shape the broader narrative and what to watch as new research comes...

headline: Exclusive-Amazon says it has reached deal with US Postal Service on package deliveries
summary: By David Shepardson WASHINGTON, April 6 (Reuters) - Amazon.com said Monday it has reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.

raw.headline: Exclusive-Amazon says it has reached deal with US Postal Service on package deliveries
raw.summary: By David Shepardson WASHINGTON, April 6 (Reuters) - Amazon.com said Monday it has reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.

headline: Amazon strikes deal with USPS that maintains 80% of package volume
summary: Amazon.com on Monday announced it reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on ‌package deliveries, and sources said the cash-strapped mail system would retain about ‌80% of its existing deliveries from its biggest customer.  That 20% cut is a dramatically better outcome for the ​postal agency than the two-thirds or larger reduction that Reuters reported last month Amazon had threatened.  USPS warned last month it could run out of cash as soon as October, and the risk that Amazon would replace the carrier by expanding its own delivery network ‌or using rivals was an ⁠existential peril.

raw.headline: Amazon strikes deal with USPS that maintains 80% of package volume
raw.summary: Amazon.com on Monday announced it reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on ‌package deliveries, and sources said the cash-strapped mail system would retain about ‌80% of its existing deliveries from its biggest customer.  That 20% cut is a dramatically better outcome for the ​postal agency than the two-thirds or larger reduction that Reuters reported last month Amazon had threatened.  USPS warned last month it could run out of cash as soon as October, and the risk that Amazon would replace the carrier by expanding its own delivery network ‌or using rivals was an ⁠existential peril.

headline: Analysis-Investors press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on water, power use in US data centers
summary: Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies are coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects.  More ‌than a dozen investors are cranking up the heat on companies ahead of annual shareholder meetings this spring, seeking more data on the ‌tech giants' water usage and conservation efforts as they seek to expand their computing power, according to interviews with Reuters.  Trillium Asset Management, a Boston-based firm with more than $4 billion in assets under ​management, filed a resolution with Alphabet in December seeking clarity on how it will meet existing climate goals given the surging energy needs of its data centers, Andrea Ranger, director of shareholder advocacy, said in an interview.

raw.headline: Analysis-Investors press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on water, power use in US data centers
raw.summary: Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies are coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects.  More ‌than a dozen investors are cranking up the heat on companies ahead of annual shareholder meetings this spring, seeking more data on the ‌tech giants' water usage and conservation efforts as they seek to expand their computing power, according to interviews with Reuters.  Trillium Asset Management, a Boston-based firm with more than $4 billion in assets under ​management, filed a resolution with Alphabet in December seeking clarity on how it will meet existing climate goals given the surging energy needs of its data centers, Andrea Ranger, director of shareholder advocacy, said in an interview.

headline: Every Major Hyperscaler Is Moving To Arm — Here's Why It Matters
summary: Counterpoint Research noted on Thursday that the AI infrastructure market is undergoing a structural shift as hyperscalers increasingly move away from Intel Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc.’s legacy x86 central processing units (CPUs) toward proprietary Arm Holdings plc Arm-based designs to optimize cost, efficiency, and control. Hyperscalers Drive Shift From x86 to Arm Major cloud players such as Alphabet Inc. Google, Amazon.com Inc. Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Corp., and Meta Platforms
